data.title: Why The Crypto.com-Sirius Partnership Could Signal New Investment Opportunities In UAE Blockchain Stocks
data.description:
The recent strategic partnership between Crypto.com and Sirius International Holding represents more than just another blockchain announcement. For investors tracking the Middle East digital asset market, this collaboration offers insight into emerging investment themes that could reshape regional technology portfolios over the next several years.

The United Arab Emirates has positioned itself as a competitive alternative to traditional crypto hubs, and institutional capital is starting to follow. When major platforms like Crypto.com commit resources to regional infrastructure partnerships, it typically signals confidence in regulatory stability and long term market viability. Investors should pay attention to how this dynamic creates opportunities across multiple asset classes.

Understanding The Investment Landscape Behind The Partnership

Sirius International Holding operates as the technology investment arm of International Holding Company (ADX: IHC), one of the largest conglomerates in the UAE with a market capitalization exceeding $238 billion. While Crypto.com itself is not publicly traded, the involvement of International Holding Company creates a direct investment angle for those seeking exposure to UAE blockchain development.

International Holding Company has built a diverse portfolio that includes energy, healthcare, real estate, and increasingly, technology infrastructure. The company’s strategic pivot toward digital assets through Sirius demonstrates how traditional Gulf capital is being reallocated toward Web3 initiatives. For investors, this suggests that blockchain adoption in the region is moving beyond speculative crypto trading into enterprise grade infrastructure that supports real economic activity.

The partnership centers on ADI Chain, a blockchain protocol designed for institutional applications. Crypto.com plans to integrate this technology into its platform, which could enhance transaction efficiency and expand the types of services available to regional users. From an investment perspective, this creates potential upside for companies positioned to benefit from increased blockchain adoption, including payment processors, financial service providers, and technology infrastructure firms operating in the UAE.

Why Regional Blockchain Infrastructure Investments Matter Now

The UAE has established regulatory frameworks through the Abu Dhabi Global Market and Dubai International Financial Centre that provide legal clarity for digital asset businesses. According to Bybit’s World Crypto Rankings released this week, the UAE ranks fifth globally for crypto adoption and has become the de facto bridge between Asia, Europe, and Africa in tokenized finance. This regulatory advantage has created a competitive moat that differentiates the UAE from other jurisdictions where crypto policy remains uncertain.

For investors, regulatory clarity translates into reduced operational risk for companies building blockchain products. When major international platforms like Crypto.com choose to partner with local entities rather than operate independently, it suggests they view the regional ecosystem as mature enough to support long term capital deployment. This type of institutional validation often precedes broader market expansion and creates opportunities for early stage investors in related sectors.

International Holding Company’s stock has gained significant attention from regional and international investors seeking exposure to UAE economic diversification. The company’s technology investments, including the Sirius division, represent a growing portion of its portfolio. While blockchain ventures remain a small segment relative to IHC’s total operations, the strategic emphasis on digital infrastructure could become a meaningful value driver as Web3 adoption accelerates across the Middle East.

Investment Themes Emerging From UAE Blockchain Growth

Several investment angles emerge from partnerships like the one between Crypto.com and Sirius. First, companies that provide blockchain infrastructure services in the region could see increased demand as more international platforms establish local operations. This includes data centers, cloud service providers, and cybersecurity firms that support digital asset platforms.

Second, financial institutions that integrate blockchain technology for cross border payments and settlement could gain competitive advantages. The UAE serves as a financial hub connecting Asia, Europe, and Africa, and blockchain based payment rails could reduce transaction costs and settlement times for regional trade. Banks and fintech companies that adopt these technologies early may capture market share from slower moving competitors.

Third, investors should monitor how traditional Gulf capital continues flowing into digital asset ventures. International Holding Company represents just one example of how sovereign wealth adjacent capital is being deployed toward Web3 infrastructure. As evidenced by Abu Dhabi’s emergence as a major crypto hub hosting multiple blockchain conferences, the region is actively courting digital asset firms. As more regional conglomerates announce similar partnerships, it creates a multiplier effect that supports ecosystem growth and potentially generates returns for shareholders.

The Crypto.com partnership with Sirius may not generate immediate headlines in Western markets, but it reflects a broader shift in where blockchain innovation is being funded and deployed. For investors willing to look beyond North American and European crypto markets, the UAE presents a compelling case study in how regulatory support and institutional capital can accelerate digital asset adoption. As these partnerships mature and more companies integrate blockchain infrastructure, the investment opportunities tied to Middle East Web3 development are likely to expand significantly.

Benzinga Disclaimer: This article is from an unpaid external contributor. It does not represent Benzinga’s reporting and has not been edited for content or accuracy.

data.title: Elon Musk A Trillionaire? A SpaceX IPO Might Put Him Shockingly Close
data.description:
Elon Musk is the richest person in the world, worth an estimated $465 billion. His wealth could more than double and reach nearly $1 trillion if SpaceX completes a planned IPO next year.

  • Tesla stock is showing weakness. Why is TSLA stock trading lower?

SpaceX IPO Could Rocket Musk’s Wealth In 2026

Musk appeared to confirm reports of a potential SpaceX IPO in 2026 with a post on social media this week.

The latest report sees SpaceX raising over $30 billion at a $1.5 trillion valuation in the largest stock market debut ever.

A SpaceX IPO could provide investors with an opportunity to invest in the space company and its future endeavors, and would also allow for more accurate calculations of Musk's wealth.

Currently valued at $465 billion, Musk's wealth is heavily tied to his stake in Tesla Inc (NASDAQ:TSLA) with estimates for his stakes in privately held companies like SpaceX, Neaurlink, xAI and more.

Bloomberg assigns a value of $136 billion to Musk's 42% stake in SpaceX. A $1.5 trillion value for SpaceX with an IPO would take Musk's stake in the company to a new value of around $625 billion, more than he is worth today.

Including his stake in Tesla and other sources of wealth, Bloomberg estimates Musk would be worth $952 billion.

Tesla recently approved a new pay package for Musk that could add nearly $1 trillion to his wealth over time if he reaches significant milestones for the electric vehicle company.

Demand for a SpaceX IPO could be huge from retail traders, and valuations for Tesla and xAI could continue to rise, making Musk’s becoming the first trillionaire a matter of when, not if.

Prediction markets show a strong likelihood that Musk will be the first trillionaire. On Kalshi, a market for who will be the first trillionaire shows the following:

  • Elon Musk: 66%
  • Mark Zuckerberg: 10%
  • Larry Page: 7%
  • Larry Ellison: 6%
  • Jensen Huang: 6%

A Kalshi market for when Elon Musk will become a trillionaire shows the following:

  • Before 2026: 2%
  • Before 2027: 33%
  • Before 2028: 60%
  • Before 2029: 50%
  • Before 2030: 50%

The prediction market shows around a third of people predicting Musk will become a trillionaire next year, with more than 50% predicting it will happen before 2028.

Read Also: SpaceX Worth $1.5 Trillion In 2026 IPO? Here’s How You Can Invest Ahead Of Public Offering

Musk's Wealth Over Time

Musk became the world's richest person in January 2021 and has held the title many times over the last five years, with some periods also seeing him fall from the top spot.

In 2022, Musk earned a Guinness World Record for the biggest drop in wealth, losing an estimated $200 billion as Tesla shares fell.

With Tesla stock soaring in late 2024, Musk became the first person to ever hit the $400 billion wealth figure.

Tesla shares are up 17.4% year-to-date in 2025, which has helped Musk's wealth increase $32.6 billion on the year.

Musk's wealth has been closely tied to Tesla’s value. A potential SpaceX IPO could better value Musk's assets and see him top the wealth chart by a significant amount.

  • Elon Musk Pay Package Compared To NFL: ‘Imagine You’re Patrick Mahomes And Not Gonna Get A Salary’

Image created using artificial intelligence via Midjourney.

data.title: GM's 'Silicon Valley Cowboy' Ignites A Stock Rally — And Tesla Finally Has Competition For The EV Spotlight
data.description:
General Motors Co (NYSE:GM) has quietly turned into 2025's most unexpected momentum story, climbing roughly 45% year to date and closing at $80.80 on Dec. 10 — its highest level since before the 2009 bankruptcy era.

  • Track GM stock here.

The stock jumping 4.7% in a single session would have been headline-worthy on its own, but the real spark is the company's renewed swagger in autonomy. And yes, that swagger has a name: Sterling Anderson, the former Tesla Inc (NASDAQ:TSLA) and Aurora Innovation Inc (NASDAQ:AUR) standout now steering GM's self-driving strategy and already shaping the narrative as the industry's "Silicon Valley cowboy."

Read Also: Jeep Parent Stellantis And Bolt Team Up To Launch Robotaxis Across Europe

GM’s Autonomy Leadership Shift

The Anderson effect arrives just as GM hits an inflection point. After trimming its EV ambitions, recalibrating production, and pushing disciplined capital allocation, the company is leaning back into autonomy — but this time with a technologist who has been on both sides of the EV–AV divide.

Investors have treated his arrival as a signal that GM is moving from cleanup mode to offense, introducing a storyline that competes directly with Tesla's long-held claim to the autonomy crown.

Wall Street Rethinks EV Winners

Goldman Sachs upgraded its price target to $93 and reaffirmed its Buy rating, pointing to stronger-than-expected third-quarter earnings and a strategic reset that's now paying off.

Wells Fargo may still be a Sell outlier, but the broader consensus has shifted toward Strong Buy territory — a rare scenario in which legacy Detroit gets more enthusiasm than Silicon Valley's EV poster child.

The dividend remains steady at $0.15 per share, padding the risk-reward calculus with something Tesla famously doesn't offer.

Tesla Comparison Creeps In

For years, Tesla dictated the tone of the EV market. Now, GM is writing a counter-narrative built on restructuring discipline, rising profitability, and an autonomy reboot under a high-profile hire.

If Anderson delivers, GM won't just be rallying — it'll be rewriting the competitive script.

  • Tesla Loses Key Bull Analyst: EV Giant Downgraded, While Rival GM Catches An Upgrade

Image created using artificial intelligence via Gemini.

data.title: Will Costco Beat Q1 Expectations? What Investors Need To Know
data.description:
Costco Wholesale Corp (NASDAQ:COST) is set to report its fiscal 2026 first-quarter earnings after the closing bell Thursday, with investors closely watching for updates on consumer spending and the retailer’s aggressive legal stance on trade tariffs. Here’s what investors need to know.

  • Costco Wholesale stock is showing downward pressure. What’s the outlook for COST shares?

What To Know: Costco shares are trading flat Thursday morning, reflecting a broader trend for the stock, which analysts note has “virtually gone nowhere this last year” and is currently down year-to-date.

Wall Street expects the warehouse giant to report first-quarter revenue of $67.17 billion, up from $62.15 billion in the same period last year. Earnings per share are projected to rise to $4.28 from $3.82.

While Costco beat expectations in its fourth-quarter report with robust comparable sales growth of 5.7%, recent sentiment has been tempered by uncertainty.

JPMorgan analyst Christopher Horvers recently lowered his price target to $1,025, maintaining an Overweight rating but flagging that November U.S. core sales were “softer than expected.”

Telsey Advisory Group's Joseph Feldman recently maintained an Outperform rating with a $1,100 target, citing Costco's strong execution despite the operating environment.

A key topic on the earnings call will likely be tariffs. Costco recently filed a lawsuit in the U.S. Court of International Trade challenging the legality of new tariffs, warning of potential margin pressures.

Why Tariffs Matter: Unlike traditional retailers that operate with gross margins of 30% or higher, Costco's business model is uniquely fragile in a high-tariff environment due to its rigid internal pricing caps. The company historically limits markups to roughly 14% for brand-name goods and 15% for its private-label Kirkland Signature items.

This razor-thin buffer means Costco cannot easily absorb tariff hikes without immediately passing that cost to members, directly threatening the value proposition on imported discretionary goods like electronics and apparel.

Benzinga Edge Rankings: Benzinga Edge data highlights a sharp divergence in the retailer’s fundamentals, pairing a weak 21.89 Momentum Score with a robust 94.88 Growth Score.

COST Price Action: Costco Wholesale shares were up 0.14% at $875.87 at the time of publication on Thursday. The stock is trading near its 52-week low of $871.09, according to Benzinga Pro data.

From a technical perspective, Costco is trading approximately 4.5% below its 50-day moving average of $917.68 and about 8.6% below its 200-day moving average of $958.94.

These indicators highlight a bearish trend, as the stock remains significantly below both key moving averages, suggesting a lack of buying interest in the near term.

Read Also: House Passes $900 Billion Defense Bill With Pay Hikes, Troop Mandates And DEI Cuts

data.title: If You Invested $1000 In First BanCorp Stock 5 Years Ago, You Would Have This Much Today
data.description:
First BanCorp (NYSE:FBP) has outperformed the market over the past 5 years by 5.84% on an annualized basis producing an average annual return of 18.98%. Currently, First BanCorp has a market capitalization of $3.32 billion.

Buying $1000 In FBP: If an investor had bought $1000 of FBP stock 5 years ago, it would be worth $2,387.32 today based on a price of $21.02 for FBP at the time of writing.

First BanCorp's Performance Over Last 5 Years

Finally -- what's the point of all this? The key insight to take from this article is to note how much of a difference compounded returns can make in your cash growth over a period of time.
